name: Stacy Kidd
credential: Chicago House DJ Legend · Grammy Award Winner
bio: One of the most versatile and skilled DJs and producers on the Chicago house scene and beyond. From Dance Mania to Defected, Ministry of Sound to his own label House 4 Life, Stacy Kidd has racked up a rich back catalogue working with Louie Vega, Frankie Knuckles, DJ Sneak, Mark Farina, Kenny Dope, Daft Punk, and many more. Starting out recording Chicago house mixes off the radio in 1987, he met Paul Johnson in 1990 and released his first record on Dance Mania in 1994. The three-time gold selling producer has produced twenty-two #1 chart-topping records and is one of the most gifted selectors around.

name: Jesse Saunders
credential: Chicago House DJ Legend · Creator of House Music
bio: Jesse Saunders is widely credited as the creator of house music. In early 1984, he and Vince Lawrence released "On and On" on Jes Say Records — recognized as one of the first house records commercially pressed to vinyl. Exposed to Chicago's nightclub scene through Frankie Knuckles at age sixteen, Saunders opened his own club, the Playground, and developed his style using loops, drum machines, and the Roland TB-303. He went on to release "Funk U Up" (the first house track to chart on Billboard), founded multiple labels, and produced the annual Chosen Few House Music Reunion Picnic drawing crowds of over 30,000.

name: Gene Hunt
credential: Chicago House DJ Legend · Grammy Award Winner
bio: Gene Hunt is truly one of Chicago's pioneers in house music since the early 80s, moving and educating crowds for over 25 years. He has played at legendary venues including The Music Box, Smart Bar, Metro, the Mid, and internationally at Dimensions Festival in Croatia, Field Maneuvers in Oxford, and venues across the UK and Scotland. Gene has worked with some of the heaviest hitters in the game — Ron Hardy, Frankie Knuckles, Farley Jack Master Funk, and Kenny Dope. His first EP "Living in the Land" was released on Traxx in 1987, followed by releases on Djax, Rush Hour, and many more.

name: Wayne Williams
credential: Chicago House DJ Legend · Grammy Award Winner
bio: Wayne Williams is the man behind the music. Over 35 years ago, he started on a journey of sound, noted for bringing disco to the Southside of Chicago and quickly becoming a local celebrity. He built "A Chosen Few" — the top booked DJ company in the area — and went on to work for Trax Records and Jive Records. His first track "Undercover" was recorded under the alias Dr. Derelict. Wayne helped bring songs like "Move Your Body" and "No Way Back" into the mainstream. Annually, the Chosen Few Picnic draws over 30,000 people. He currently hosts "Two Nations" monthly in New York with Kenny Dope.

name: DJ Heather
bio: DJ Heather is one of Chicago's most respected house music selectors, known for her versatility and deep connection with the dancefloor. She has held residencies at iconic venues including Smart Bar, The Mid, and Spybar, and performed alongside Derrick Carter, Mark Farina, DJ Sneak, and Diz. Her sets have taken her to festivals and clubs worldwide — from Winter Music Conference and Movement in Detroit to stages in London, Paris, Berlin, and Barcelona. A tireless champion of emerging talent, DJ Heather's dedication to the craft and the community has made her one of the premier selectors in the nation.

name: Diz
bio: Born and raised in Chicago, Diz's adolescence coincided with the beginnings of the city's legendary house scene. At 14, his brother took him to The Music Box, where he witnessed the true potential of house music. He broke into the scene at 19 after meeting Mark Farina, Derrick Carter, and Gemini, holding residencies at Shelter and Red Dog alongside Johnny Fiasco. In 1995, he joined Chez Damier's Prescription Records and began producing with Joshua as Iz n Diz, releasing on Classic, Guidance, and Silver Network. Known for his insane DJ skills, stage presence, and warm-hearted personality.

name: Miles Maeda
bio: Miles Maeda began as a college radio DJ in 1988. Moving to Chicago in the early 90's, he helped shape the rave and club scene, eventually holding his own 11-year residency at Smartbar. After moving to San Diego, he expanded his yoga and meditation work, traveling extensively and living in Tokyo for 7 years — giving birth to the eclectic ZenDaze party and the blossoming of Total M edits. Miles has recently moved back to Southern California to continue both his music productions and ongoing yoga education.

name: Hector Moralez
bio: Rising from Oakland, California, Hector Moralez has paid his dues working tirelessly from the bottom up. His ambitions began at age 17, spending hours practicing with vinyl from the infamous Primal Records. His sounds take influence from Latin, jazz, funk, hip hop, and soul, sewn together with grooving bass lines and solid fat beats. Releases on Music For Freaks, Robsoul, DJ Sneak Classics, and Magnetic, plus a debut album with Chris Carrier on Apollonia in 2013. Acclaimed by Derrick Carter, DJ Sneak, Doc Martin, Mark Farina, Diz, and DJ Heather.

name: Wally Callerio
bio: With over 20 years behind the decks and in the studio, Wally Callerio is no stranger to the world of house music. Part DJ, producer, and label mainstay, his accolades include over 100 original tracks and countless remixes released on over 40 labels internationally. Callerio's musical journey began in the early 90's on the West Coast — melodic vocals and chunky basslines remain the cornerstone of his sound, and his global following continues to grow organically along with him.

name: Pete Moss
bio: Following his beginnings providing soundtracks for sunrises up and down the East Coast, Pete Moss settled in Philly alongside King Britt, Josh Wink, and the Ovum crew. He developed into one of dance music's finest talents with his late night sets and impeccable soulful production. From blowing minds at Ministry of Sound UK to shaking things up at Pacha Ibiza and fabric London, Pete has traveled the world more times than he can count. Releases on Dessous, Ovum, Nordic Trax, Plastic City, and his own Recline imprint.

name: Brett Johnson
bio: With influences ranging from early house and disco to turntablism and techno, Brett Johnson straddles the line between knowledgeable selector and genuine floor-filler. Releasing his first record in 1999, Brett has put out over 100 records and remixes for Crosstown Rebels, Classic Music Company, 20/20 Vision, Get Physical, Visionquest, Magnetic, Freerange, Cynosure, and F-Communications. His sound is best known as groovy, bumping house.

name: Kevin Yost
bio: Ever since picking up drumsticks at age four, Kevin Yost has been obsessed with creating and performing music. In the mid 80s he discovered DJing and house music, and in 1998 broke through with his first album One Starry Night — considered a deep house classic. With close to 100 single releases, 8 albums, and nearly 1500 unreleased tracks, Kevin is known for playing all-original music sets and his "Live & Improvised" show where all music is created on the spot. Each gig is completely different, tailored live to take the crowd on a journey.

name: Lady Monix
bio: LADYMONIX is a Baltimore-native producer and DJ based in Detroit, known for turning out dancefloors with her deep disco and house sets. She's performed at Pittsburgh's Hot Mass, Brooklyn's House of Yes, and the darkest Detroit warehouse afterhours. Production credits include remixes, edits, and original work on her label Frizner Electric. Her 2019 EP 'Baby' earned "Top Tune of the Month" from DJ Mag, and she contributed an exclusive track to Detroit Love Vol. 3, curated by Waajeed on Planet E.

name: John Simmons
bio: John Simmons is a veteran Chicago DJ with more than 30 years on the decks. With roots in soul, funk, disco, and golden era hip-hop, John caught the Chicago house music bug in the early 90s and has championed that sound ever since. He has shared the stage with Frankie Knuckles, Cajmere, Kaytranada, and more, and has run the successful "Strictly 90s" party for over ten years alongside Gant-Man and Czboogie, featuring legends like Mike Dunn, DJ Sneak, and Terry Hunter. John hosts SOULTRONIC on Lumpen Radio Chicago 105.5 FM and holds production credits with the late great Paul Johnson, Geto Mark, and Tevo Howard. A true Chicago ambassador who stays true to past influences while constantly pushing the culture forward.

name: Mark E. Quark
bio: With an ability to mesmerize audiences over radio waves, internet connections, and any dancefloor, Mark E. Quark has lit up audiences all over the world. He has played alongside Doc Martin, Derrick Carter, Louie Vega, Juan Atkins, Mark Farina, Richard James (Aphex Twin), and Moby. His handwriting can be seen all over Southern California's legendary nightlife scene — a pivotal force in the foundation of SOMA, Playskool, and G-Force, with releases on Hi-Phen (Belgium), Camouflage, and Facade. His style — groovy, laid back, and nasty — draws from DJ Harvey, Idjut Boys, Jeno, and Andrew Weatherall, mashed up into something totally unique, currently brought to life at Moonshake in San Diego.

name: Doc Link
bio: Inspired by the legendary club sets of Farley "Jackmaster" Funk, Gene Hunt, and Ron Hardy, Doc Link launched his DJ career in 1987. His versatility across soul, house, and raw funk allowed him to develop a signature style — mixing new and old, often including his own re-edits. A winning collaboration with Roy Davis Jr. on "Dance Shake" propelled Doc into the limelight, and his production alliance with Malik Alston on the classic "Back 2 Chicago" on King Street cemented his place in house music. In 2006 he co-founded Liberate Recordings with EMan. Behind the decks, Doc Link's sets are energetic, raw, and completely from the soul.
